channel.title: AI Thought Leader Talks (YouTube)
channel.description: Original long-form talks and interviews with AI thought leaders, filtered by Claude.

item.title: [Jensen Huang] Jensen Huang: Nvidia's Future, Physical AI, Rise of the Agent, Inference Explosion, AI PR Crisis
item.author: All-In Podcast
item.description:
All-In Podcast · 66 min

Jensen Huang joins the All-In Podcast to discuss Nvidia's acquisition of Groq and the inference explosion, decision-making at the world's most valuable company, Physical AI's $50T market, OpenClaw's future, AI's PR crisis and refuting doomer narratives, Anthropic's communications mistakes, revenue capacity and token allocation for employees, Karpathy's autoresearch and the agentic future, open source and global diffusion, Iran/Taiwan supply chain impact, self-driving platform and facing competition, datacenters in space, AI healthcare, robotics, OpenAI/Anthropic revenue potential, how to build an AI moat, and advice to young people on excelling in the AI era.

item.title: [Jensen Huang] Morgan Stanley TMT Conference 2026 | Jensen Huang on AI, Compute, Tokens and the New Global Economy
item.author: Morgan Stanley
item.description:
Morgan Stanley · 43 min

From the Morgan Stanley Technology, Media & Telecom Conference in San Francisco, NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang explains how AI is reshaping the global economy. His core insight: Compute creates tokens, tokens power intelligence, and intelligence translates into economic output—at every level, from companies to countries.

item.title: [Jensen Huang] NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang GTC 2026 Full Keynote
item.author: Yahoo Finance
item.description:
Yahoo Finance · 140 min

Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang delivered his annual GTC keynote in San Jose, California. Nvidia has been on a dealmaking spree, including an acqui-hire agreement with AI inference chip designer Groq. Nvidia's strategic partner Nebius reached a deal with Meta to provide the hyperscaler with up to $27 billion worth of capacity starting in 2027.

item.title: [Jensen Huang] LIVE: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang Unveils AI Innovations | DRM News | AI1F
item.author: DRM News
item.description:
DRM News · 189 min

Jensen Huang delivers the keynote address at Nvidia's AI conference in San Jose, unveiling the latest advancements in artificial intelligence, GPU technologies, and AI-driven innovations shaping the future of computing.

item.title: [Org: Anthropic Talks] Why Anthropic Thinks AI Should Have Its Own Computer — Felix Rieseberg of Claude Cowork/Code
item.author: Latent Space
item.description:
Latent Space · 88 min

Felix Rieseberg, who previously built Electron and helped ship the Slack desktop app, now shapes Claude Cowork at Anthropic. He unpacks how Claude Cowork emerged from Anthropic's prototype-first culture, why execution has suddenly become cheap enough that teams can just build all the candidates, and why the real frontier in AI products is no longer better chat but trusted task execution. Topics include: why Silicon Valley is undervaluing the local computer, the VM as both a safety boundary and a capability unlock for Claude, how Cowork differs from Claude Code in evals and planning horizons, skills vs. MCPs, real use cases already happening today (uploading videos, organizing files, handling taxes, managing calendars, debugging crashes, analyzing finances), Anthropic's bias toward integrating with Chrome and Office, computer use one year later and vision plus browser context, the future of junior work and labor disruption, and the agentic future of the desktop.

item.title: [Org: OpenAI Talks] Building AI for better healthcare — the OpenAI Podcast Ep. 14
item.author: OpenAI
item.description:
OpenAI · 31 min

OpenAI Head of Health Dr. Nate Gross and Karan Singhal, who leads Health AI Research, discuss how AI can help address the biggest challenges in healthcare systems around the world. They cover how OpenAI is training models to handle sensitive health questions in collaboration with physicians, and how that foundation is unlocking a new generation of tools for patients, clinicians, and healthcare systems. Topics include: the origins of their interest in AI and healthcare, strategy for building AI tools for clinicians, how AI models are trained for health use cases, how OpenAI scores well on health evals, key challenges deploying AI in healthcare, collaboration with hospitals and healthcare systems, practical everyday uses of AI health assistants, and feedback from clinicians and early users.

item.title: [Org: Google DeepMind Talks] Navigating the Future of AI with Logan Kilpatrick | Google Deepmind
item.author: Rishab in Cloud
item.description:
Rishab in Cloud · 45 min

Logan Kilpatrick from Google DeepMind discusses the evolving landscape of AI development, particularly focusing on the transition from traditional roles to more integrated positions within AI teams. They explore the importance of community engagement, maximizing developer agency, and the implications of the Gemini 3.1 release. The discussion also touches on the future of software engineering, the evolution of Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG), and the impact of AI tools on software development careers and open source contributions.

item.title: [Org: Google DeepMind Talks] Agent AI- What is Your Mission?
item.author: The Human in the Loop
item.description:
The Human in the Loop · 37 min

A discussion on AI agents and the question of what we need to think about as we enter a new era of machine intelligence. Why should there always be a human in the loop? Guests include Raia Hadsell, VP of Research at Google DeepMind, and Michael Wooldridge, Ashall Professor of the Foundations of Artificial Intelligence at the University of Oxford.

item.title: [Channel: Dwarkesh Patel] Terence Tao – How the world’s top mathematician uses AI
item.author: Dwarkesh Patel
item.description:
Dwarkesh Patel · 84 min

The episode begins with the ingenious way Kepler discovered the laws of planetary motion. The conversation explores whether AI will make fast progress in scientific discovery due to tight verification loops, and why the story of how we discovered the shape of our solar system shows that verification loops for correct ideas can be decades or millennia long. Topics include: selection bias in reported AI discoveries, why AI makes papers richer and broader but not deeper, if AI solves a problem can humans get understanding from it, the need for a semi-formal language for how scientists actually talk to each other, how Terry uses his time, and why human-AI hybrids will dominate math for a long while.

item.title: [Channel: Y Combinator] How AI Is Unlocking Millions Of New Builders
item.author: Y Combinator
item.description:
Y Combinator · 40 min

Mukund and Madhav Jha, founders of Emergent—an AI platform that lets anyone build and ship production-ready software—discuss how in just eight months users have created more than 7 million apps on Emergent, with the number doubling in the last 45 days. They explain how they built one of the most powerful AI coding agents, why they focused on non-technical users, and what it's like building for a global audience from India. Topics include: the pivot from AI testing to general coding agents, why second movers can win in AI, building for production not just prototypes, live demo of building apps with Emergent, how Emergent hires and runs a lean team, is SaaS dead with the rise of personalized software, and the future of niche apps, solo builders and AI agency.

item.title: [Channel: Zhang Xiaojun Podcast] 133. 对谢赛宁的7小时马拉松访谈：世界模型、逃出硅谷、AMI Labs、两次拒绝Ilya、杨立昆、李飞飞和42
item.author: Zhang Xiaojun Podcast
item.description:
Zhang Xiaojun Podcast · 406 min

In February 2026, during Chinese New Year celebrations with humanoid robots performing on stage in China and a brutal blizzard hitting New York, the conversation with Saining Xie began in a cluttered Brooklyn apartment. By March 2026, Xie, alongside Turing Award laureate Yann LeCun, embarked on a venture into World Models. Their company AMI Labs (Advanced Machine Intelligence Labs) consists of just 25 people, has raised $1.03B in seed funding with a $3.5B pre-money valuation, with no product. Xie argues Silicon Valley is deeply trapped in LLMs and completely sedated by them. Born in 1990, this Chinese-American mathematician graduated from Shanghai Jiao Tong University and UC Berkeley, and is now a professor at NYU. Before entrepreneurship, he was a research scientist at Google DeepMind, and before that spent 4 years as a research scientist at Meta's FAIR. His papers have over 100,000 citations. Topics include: Xie and LeCun's collaboration founding AMI, the World Models research direction, why Silicon Valley is LLM-pilled, research taste and the Gold Rush, what World Models are, and the future of AI.
